violations.description: Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Different species of raw animal food was not properly separated from each other or arranged according to minimum cooking temperatures. -Tube of raw hamburger stored with package of raw meat and packages of raw steaks with container and grocery package of raw chicken stored on shelf above the beef items.--Raw animal food was not properly separated from raw or cooked ready-to-eat food. -Raw bison tubes in pan with ziploc bag of parmesan. *Items disposed of while on-site.

Refrigerated Storage. — --Refrigerator or cold storage was insufficient or ineffective at maintaining potentially hazardous food at a temperature of 41F or less. -Upright white refrigerator found with 44F at top to 50F at the bottom. *Build up of ice at drain pan and fan not operating.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. -Some crumb debris at sheet pans in speed rack. -Debris at interior of oven and at tongs at oven handle.

Manual cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Sanitizer solution for three compartment sink was not maintained at the proper concentration. -Sani Bin used for dishes found cloudy and at 0ppm Chlorine dumped by staff and refreshed to >200ppm. *COS-dumped, washed and rinsed bin and then showed staff to use ~1 cap of bleach per full bin of water for 50ppm chlorine solution.--Three compartment sink was not maintained or operated in the approved three step method. -3rd sink is blocked covered with wire shelf and used as drain rack. -Sani Bin being used as 3rd compartment sink is also being used as a sani bucket. *COS-made a separate container to use as a sani bucket while on-site.

Sanitizing solution testing devices. — --Test strip or kit that accurately measures the sanitizing concentration was not provided. -Test strips were not available in kitchen. Staff currently use bleach but may use Quat in the future. *Ensure test strips are available for whichever chemical is used in-house.
